confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_19b837f5-59ce-4cb3-8ed7-2efa1f12129f read back as received

evidence: Staccato was healthy and quiescent, but this required separate status, ops, check, and drafts commands to establish.
The local residual task initially described the four-command inspection path; operator correctly pushed that the desired surface should be a CLI command, not remembered manual choreography.
The local Staccato task should belong to narada-staccato.builder, while the generic capability belongs to narada-proper.architect/product machinery.
proposal: Add canonical Site-level commands such as narada site health, narada site work-next, and/or narada site stabilize that work across Site kinds and execution surfaces.

The command should summarize sync freshness, daemon posture, active/retryable/stuck work, pending drafts, failed-terminal attention, mailbox/runtime readiness, known residuals, and next recommended action.
Local Site wrappers such as pnpm health should be thin projections of the canonical command, not independent reinventions.
payload.recommendation: Treat this as execution work for Narada proper: design and implement a canonical cross-Site posture/work-next surface, then let Staccato use it instead of growing a private convention.
payload.summary: While checking the Staccato Narada Site, the available local posture required running and mentally reconciling pnpm status, pnpm ops, pnpm check, and pnpm drafts. This is technically CLI-based but ergonomically incoherent for routine Site operation. Each Site should not have to invent its own wrapper for daemon posture, sync freshness, work queue, drafts, failed-terminal attention, runtime readiness, residuals, and next recommended action.
payload.title: Narada Sites need canonical posture and work-next operator commands
